info.description:
REST API for accessing blockchain data including blocks, transactions,
subtrees, UTXOs, and merkle proofs. Supports JSON, hexadecimal, and
binary response formats.

BlockExtended.description:
This structure extends the standard Block model to provide additional blockchain traversal
capabilities by including a reference to the next block in the chain. This is particularly
useful for blockchain explorers and analysis tools that need to navigate the chain in
a forward direction.

The structure preserves all fields from the base Block model through embedding, while
adding the NextBlock field that can be used for chain traversal. This approach maintains
compatibility with existing Block-based code while extending functionality.

Fields:
Block: Embedded Block model containing all standard block data including header, transactions, etc.
NextBlock: Hash of the next block in the chain, or nil if this is the tip of the chain
BlockExtended.title:
BlockExtended represents a block with additional information about the next block
in the blockchain. It embeds the base Block model and adds the next block hash.

nonce.description: Nonce used to generate the block.

timestamp.description: Time the block was created im unix time.

version.description: Version of the block.  This is not the same as the protocol version.

ChainParamsResponse.title: ChainParamsResponse contains the blockchain network parameters.

ExtendedResponse.description: It's used for endpoints that return lists of items requiring pagination.
ExtendedResponse.title: ExtendedResponse wraps API response data with pagination information.

data.description: Data contains the actual response payload

FeeAmount.title: FeeAmount represents the mining fee as satoshis per bytes.

GetBlockHeightResponse.description: GetBlockHeightResponse provides the current block height

GetMedianBlockTimeResponse.description: GetMedianBlockTimeResponse provides the median time of recent blocks

Hash.description:
Hash is used in several of the bitcoin messages and common structures.  It
typically represents the double sha256 of data.

Input.description: DO NOT CHANGE ORDER - Optimized for memory via maligned
Input.title: Input is a representation of a transaction input

LegacyMerkleProofResponse.title: LegacyMerkleProofResponse is kept for reference but no longer used.

metadata.description: define a map of string to string

Output.description: Output is a representation of a transaction output

Pagination.description: It provides information about the current page and total available records.
Pagination.title: Pagination represents pagination metadata for API responses that return lists of items.

limit.description: Limit indicates the maximum number of items per page

offset.description: Offset indicates the starting position in the full dataset

totalRecords.description: TotalRecords indicates the total number of available records

PeerInfoResponse.title: PeerInfoResponse represents the JSON response for a single peer.

PeersResponse.title: PeersResponse represents the JSON response containing all peers.

Policy.title: Policy contains the node's policy settings in ARC format.

PolicyResponse.title: PolicyResponse is the response format for GET /v1/policy.

ResetReputationRequest.title: ResetReputationRequest represents the JSON request body.

SettingMetadata.title: SettingMetadata contains metadata about a single configuration setting.

SettingsResponse.title: SettingsResponse represents the API response for settings.

SpendingData.title: SpendingData contains information about a transaction that spends a UTXO.

vin.description: Vin is the input index in the spending transaction

SubtreeMeta.title: SubtreeMeta represents metadata for a single subtree within a block.

SubtreeTx.title: SubtreeTx represents a transaction within a subtree.

Timestamp.title:
A Timestamp represents a point in time independent of any time zone or local
calendar, encoded as a count of seconds and fractions of seconds at
nanosecond resolution. The count is relative to an epoch at UTC midnight on
January 1, 1970, in the proleptic Gregorian calendar which extends the
Gregorian calendar backwards to year one.

nanos.description:
Non-negative fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution. This field is
the nanosecond portion of the duration, not an alternative to seconds.
Negative second values with fractions must still have non-negative nanos
values that count forward in time. Must be between 0 and 999,999,999
inclusive.

seconds.description:
Represents seconds of UTC time since Unix epoch 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z. Must
be between -315576000000 and 315576000000 inclusive (which corresponds to
0001-01-01T00:00:00Z to 9999-12-31T23:59:59Z).

Tx.description: DO NOT CHANGE ORDER - Optimized memory via malign
Tx.title: Tx wraps a bitcoin transaction

UTXOItem.description: It contains both the output details and its current status in the UTXO set.
UTXOItem.title: UTXOItem represents detailed information about an Unspent Transaction Output (UTXO).

lockTime.description:
LockTime specifies when this UTXO becomes spendable.
Only present if the UTXO has a time lock.

satoshis.description: Satoshis represents the amount of this output in satoshis

status.description: Status indicates the current state of the UTXO (e.g., "SPENT", "NOT_FOUND")

vout.description: Vout is the index of this output in the creating transaction

ValidateTransactionBatchRequest.description: ValidateTransactionBatchRequest contains multiple transactions for batch validation

ValidateTransactionRequest.description: ValidateTransactionRequest contains data for transaction validation

blockHeaderResponse.description:
blockHeaderResponse represents a formatted block header response that includes
additional metadata beyond the basic block header information. It embeds the
BlockHeader model and adds fields for presentation purposes.

nonce.description: Nonce used to generate the block.

timestamp.description: Time the block was created im unix time.

version.description: Version of the block.  This is not the same as the protocol version.

errorResponse.description: It provides a consistent format that includes both HTTP and application-level error details.
errorResponse.title: errorResponse defines the standard error response structure used across all API endpoints.

code.description: Code contains the application-specific error code

error.description: Err contains the human-readable error message

status.description: Status contains the HTTP status code

forks.description: It encapsulates the entire fork hierarchy starting from a given block.
forks.title: forks represents the root structure for the block fork tree response.

forksLink.description: Used for visual representation of block relationships.
forksLink.title: forksLink represents connection information between blocks in the fork tree.

forksTree.description: It contains block information and maintains parent-child relationships.
forksTree.title: forksTree represents a node in the block fork tree structure.

blockHeaderListResponse.description: Block header list in JSON format

extendedResponse.description: Paginated response with data and pagination metadata
